title: afzelia pinnatifida
intro: Afzelia pinnatifida, also known as the Astringent Tree or the Malabar kino tree, is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Afzelia pinnatifida include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. Afzelia pinnatifida is typically prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion for use in herbal medicine.
